notes: Per-sensor info for the gauge dashboard tap-to-info dialogs. Each entry: title, what (description), normal (typical range), warn (warning condition), help (why it matters).

RPM.title: Engine Speed (RPM)
RPM.what: How many revolutions per minute the crankshaft is making. Measured by the crank position sensor.
RPM.normal: Idle: 600-750 rpm warm. Cruise: 1500-2500 rpm. Highway: 2000-3000 rpm. Redline N52B30: ~6700 rpm.
RPM.warn: Above 6500 rpm is the redline zone — engine damage risk if held there. Below 500 rpm at idle = stalling risk, dirty throttle body or vacuum leak.
RPM.help: Use to verify your transmission is shifting normally, check for sticky cold-start idle, and spot stretched timing chain symptoms (irregular idle rpm hunting).

Vehicle Speed.title: Vehicle Speed
Vehicle Speed.what: Speed of the car in km/h. On E65 this comes from the DSC (ABS) module wheel speed sensors, not a single VSS.
Vehicle Speed.warn: Frequent dropouts to 0 while moving = DSC wheel speed sensor failing (DTC 5DF0-5DF3).
Vehicle Speed.help: Used by the gearbox for shift logic, cruise control, traction control. If unreliable, all those systems behave badly.

Engine Load.title: Engine Load (calculated)
Engine Load.what: Percentage of maximum airflow the engine is currently using. Computed by the DME from MAF / throttle / RPM.
Engine Load.normal: Idle: 15-25 %. Cruise on highway: 25-45 %. Hard acceleration: 70-95 %. WOT: 90-100 %.
Engine Load.warn: Stuck high at idle = vacuum leak, dirty MAF, or fuel trim problem. Stuck low under throttle = MAF reading wrong.
Engine Load.help: Single best diagnostic for 'feels gutless' or 'rough idle' — compare actual load vs throttle position to spot intake leaks.

Throttle Position.title: Throttle Position
Throttle Position.what: Where the drive-by-wire throttle plate is positioned. 0% = closed, 100% = wide open.
Throttle Position.normal: Idle: 12-18 % (idle bypass is added). Cruise: 15-25 %. WOT: 80-100 %.
Throttle Position.warn: Sudden drop to 0% during driving = throttle body sensor fault (P0120-P0123). Stuck reading = sensor potentiometer worn.
Throttle Position.help: DK-Motor (throttle body) failure is common on N52 above 150k km. €400-600 replacement. Reading + Pedal Position discrepancy = drive-by-wire fault.

Coolant Temp.title: Coolant Temperature
Coolant Temp.what: Temperature of the engine coolant at the thermostat housing. Drives fan, fuel enrichment, and thermal protection.
Coolant Temp.normal: Cold start: 20-40 °C. Warming: rising steadily. Warmed up: 85-105 °C is normal operating range. Thermostat opens at ~88 °C.
Coolant Temp.warn: Above 110 °C = overheating, pull over. Below 75 °C when warm = stuck-open thermostat (P0128, fuel economy hit). Above 95 °C in stop-go traffic = electric water pump weakening.
Coolant Temp.help: The most safety-critical reading. N52 water pump fails at 100-130k km — usually with no warning until you see steam. Watch for slow warm-up + cooling in traffic.

Oil Temp.title: Oil Temperature
Oil Temp.what: Engine oil temperature, measured in the oil sump.
Oil Temp.normal: From cold: warms slower than coolant — takes 10-15 min driving. Steady-state: 95-115 °C. High load / hot ambient: up to 125 °C.
Oil Temp.warn: Above 130 °C = oil breaking down, back off the throttle. Reading 50 °C below coolant after 15 min driving = sensor fault.
Oil Temp.help: Critical in GCC heat. Above 130 °C accelerates sludge formation and bearing wear. Don't redline if oil is cold (under 80 °C).

Intake Air Temp.title: Intake Air Temperature
Intake Air Temp.what: Temperature of air entering the engine, measured at the MAF sensor inlet.
Intake Air Temp.normal: Cold start: matches ambient. Driving: 10-25 °C above ambient. Stopped after driving (heat soak): 50-80 °C briefly.
Intake Air Temp.warn: Above 65 °C while driving = heat soak in engine bay, performance reduction. Should drop when airspeed picks up.
Intake Air Temp.help: Cars in GCC see high IAT routinely. High IAT = DME retards timing for knock protection = power loss. Cold air intake helps if your IAT runs hot.

Mass Air Flow.title: Mass Air Flow (MAF)
Mass Air Flow.what: Mass of air entering the engine per second, measured by the hot-film MAF sensor at the air filter outlet.
Mass Air Flow.normal: Idle: 3-5 g/s. Cruise: 10-25 g/s. WOT N52B30: 90-120 g/s.
Mass Air Flow.warn: Way too low under load = dirty/failing MAF (P0100-P0103) or intake leak. Implausible reading vs throttle = swap-test or clean MAF.
Mass Air Flow.help: Critical for fuel calculation. Dirty MAF causes lean codes (P0171), rough idle, and gutless feel. Clean with MAF spray (NOT carb cleaner) every 50k km.

Battery Voltage.title: Battery Voltage
Battery Voltage.what: Voltage across the battery terminals as seen by the OBD adapter (reads via ATRV).
Battery Voltage.normal: Engine off: 12.4-12.7 V (healthy). Engine running: 13.8-14.4 V (alternator charging).
Battery Voltage.warn: Below 12.0 V at rest = weak battery. Above 14.7 V running = overcharging (voltage regulator). Below 13.5 V running = alternator weak or battery not registered.
Battery Voltage.help: GCC heat kills batteries at 5-7 years (vs 8-10 in Europe). When you replace, MUST register the new battery via the Service Functions screen, otherwise alternator under-charges.

Fuel Level.title: Fuel Level
Fuel Level.what: Percentage of fuel remaining in the tank. On E65 this comes from two senders (saddle tank) — one each side.
Fuel Level.normal: Drops linearly with consumption. E65 tank ~88 L.
Fuel Level.warn: Jumpy reading around the 50% mark = one of the twin senders failing. Stays full or empty = sender stuck.
Fuel Level.help: Twin-sender tank means accuracy varies as the saddle empties. Low fuel light typically comes on at ~10 L remaining.
